content.claim: The number of children identified as SNI (special educational needs) in Hungary grew from 92,800 to 97,300 in a single year (2021/2022), representing the largest year-on-year increase in at least 12 years, and the 2023/2024 figure reached approximately 63,000 or 8.8% of primary-school pupils, with a 40% rise over the preceding 20 years.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Hungarian education system lens
limitations: Figures come from journalistic aggregation rather than a single unified official time series
Category definitions and boundaries may have shifted across reporting years
Base-year comparability is not independently verified in these sources
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
